title: Fix infinite loading screen — app stuck on 'Loading your profile...'

requirements.text: Fix the infinite 'Loading your profile...' state in App.tsx. The app is stuck showing a loading spinner and never resolves to either the login screen or the main UI. Investigate the profile-fetching logic in useQueries.ts (useGetProfile or equivalent hook) — add a timeout or error boundary so that if the profile query fails, errors out, or returns null/undefined after a reasonable timeout (e.g., 5 seconds), the app falls back gracefully to the unauthenticated login state instead of spinning forever. Ensure the loading guard in App.tsx does not block rendering when the actor or identity is not yet ready.

quotes: Am not able to login it is still loading same as in snips.. It is okay me to lose data but do fix

acceptanceCriteria: Opening the app no longer results in an indefinite 'Loading your profile...' spinner.
If the profile query fails or times out, the app falls back to the login/unauthenticated state within 5 seconds.
After the fallback, the user can see and interact with the login button to authenticate via Internet Identity.
No runtime errors are thrown during the loading-to-login fallback transition.

requirements.text: Reset or clear any corrupted stable state in the backend Motoko actor (backend/main.mo) that may be causing the profile lookup to hang or fail on canister call. The user has explicitly consented to data loss. Drop and reinitialize all stable variables (expenses, userProfiles, or equivalent) to empty collections so the canister starts fresh and responds correctly to profile queries.

quotes: It is okay me to lose data but do fix

acceptanceCriteria: The backend canister responds successfully to getProfile (or equivalent) calls without hanging.
All stable state is initialized to empty on upgrade — no leftover records from previous versions.
The frontend can complete the profile-fetch round-trip and render the app correctly after the backend reset.

constraints: Do not modify any file listed under frontend.immutablePaths (useInternetIdentity.ts, useActor.ts, main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui).
Data loss is explicitly accepted by the user — stable variables may be reset to empty.
nonGoals: Preserving or migrating any existing expense or profile data.
Adding new features or UI changes unrelated to the login/loading fix.
Changing the Internet Identity authentication flow itself.
